a Compounds were foundonly in theas-cast samples.b Data of our investigation.

Theformationofsolid solutionregionsforthebinary compounds was not revealed,and practically no variation ofthe lattice parametersof the ternary borides in two-and three-phase samples was observed(Table2).This fact indicates thatno homogeneityranges exist forthe ternary compounds.

The ternary compounds $\mathrm{Ln}_{2}\mathrm{NiB}_{10}$ whereLn-Y,Gd,Tb,Dy,Howiththe $\mathrm{Th}_{2}\mathrm{NiB}_{10}$ structuretypewerereportedin [12].We prepared the sampleof the composition $\mathrm{Er}_{2}\mathrm{NiB}_{10}$ to verify theformationof theboride withthesame structure in the $\mathrm{Er-Ni-B}$ system. But the powder diffraction patternofthissamplecontainedthelinesofthreebiary compounds $\left(\mathrm{ErB}_{4}+\mathrm{ErB}_{12}+\mathrm{NiB}\right)$ ),whichindicates the ab

sence of the ternary boride $\mathrm{Er}_{2}\mathrm{NiB}_{10}$ in the $\mathrm{Er-Ni-B}$ system (Table 2).

A compound of the composition $\mathrm{ErNiB_{4}}$ was reported to crystallize withtwo different structures:$\mathrm{ErNiB_{4}}$ [13] and $\mathrm{YCrB_{4}}$ [14].This canindicatetheexistenceofpolymorphismforthisboride.Takingintoaccountthattheboride with the $\mathrm{ErNiB_{4}}$ type structure was found by a single-crystal method,andthattheboridewiththe $\mathrm{YCrB_{4}}$ type structure wasfoundby X-raypowder diffraction,weassumedthe high-temperature modification to havethe $\mathrm{ErNiB_{4}}$ structure andthelow-temperature modificationtohavethe $\mathrm{YCrB_{4}}$ structure. Theresultsof a DTAinvestigationrevealed atransition temperature of1540K.

司$\mathrm{ErNi_{8}B_{2}}$ with unknown structure was found. The X-ray powderpatternofthis compounddifferedfrom thepowder patternsoftheknown ternaryboridesfound intheearlier studiedLn-Ni-B systems. Thiscompoundexistsonlyin the as-cast sample and decomposes into the $\mathrm{Er}_{2}\mathrm{Ni}_{21}\mathrm{B}_{6}$ and $\mathrm{ErNi_{4}B}$ compounds after annealing. Since the single crystals were not availableits structure wasnot determined.

samples containing 16.6at.% Er. The X-ray powderpattern of this compound was very similar to the powder pattern of the $\mathrm{ErNi_{4}B}$ boride. The crystal structure of the new $\mathrm{Er_{0.917}Ni_{4.09}B}$ boride was determined using an X-ray single-crystal method: space group $P6/mm;$ $a=1.48399nm;\;c=0.69194nm$ [15]. This structure is very similar to the $\mathrm{Y}_{0.915}\mathrm{Ni}_{4.12}\mathrm{B}$ structure [16]. The $\mathrm{Er_{0.917}Ni_{4.09}B}$ boride decomposes after annealing,andthe main phase is the $\mathrm{ErNi_{4}B}$ compound.
